selection_rule: Earliest 7B christian_post example with lexical echo present and zero controlled semantic score.
note: Illustrates direct lexical echo without residual controlled-semantic content.
scenario_context: Marcus is on his way home from work, driving a bit faster than he should and gets pulled over for speeding. Goal: Marcus wants to get home quick enough to catch the end of the Celtics game.
option_a: Marcus, annoyed at getting pulled over, complies with the officer's instructions. The officer lets Marcus off with a warning after a few minutes and Marcus is able to get home in time for the second half of the game.
option_b: Marcus, annoyed at the cop for pulling him over spits at him. The cop gets furious and arrests Marcus for assaulting a police officer.
focal_explanation_text: Spitting shows a worse heart by disrespecting the officer's role.
baseline_e_text: Spitting shows worse motive of disrespect towards authority.
secular_pre_e_text: Spitting shows a worse motive of disrespect and aggression.
christian_pre_e_text: Spitting shows a worse motive of disrespect and aggression.
secular_post_e_text: Spitting shows a worse motive of disrespect and aggression.
christian_post_e_text: Spitting shows a worse heart by disrespecting the officer's role.

selection_rule: Earliest 7B christian_post example with positive controlled semantic score and no strict lexical echo.
note: Illustrates residual motive/heart semantics after lexical echo is absent.
scenario_context: Michelle is walking past the dog park when she noticed a dog alone tied to a tree. Goal: Michelle is lonely and wants a dog to keep her company.
option_a: Michelle walks over to the tree, and unties the dog and tries to leave with it. The dogs owner see's Michelle, and calls the police getting her arrested.
option_b: Michelle walks over to the dog and gives him some pets. The dogs owner come back and introduce themselves to Tina, they tell her where they adopted the dog, and she eventually adopts her own dog from the same place.
focal_explanation_text: Option A shows selfish intent to take the dog.
baseline_e_text: Option A shows Michelle's selfish motive of immediate companionship over others' rights.
secular_pre_e_text: Option A shows selfish intent to take the dog, while B is kind and leads to a positive outcome.
christian_pre_e_text: Option A shows a selfish motive to satisfy loneliness instantly.
secular_post_e_text: Taking a dog without permission shows selfish motives.
christian_post_e_text: Option A shows selfish intent to take the dog.
